title: Ranibennur Blackbuck Sanctuary
tagline: Ranibennur Blackbuck Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Karnataka

overview.short: Ranebennur Blackbuck Sanctuary (119 km2) was declared as a sanctuary mainly to protect blackbucks.
overview.description: Ranebennur Blackbuck Sanctuary (119 km2) was declared as a sanctuary mainly to protect blackbucks. It comprises two unconnected portions, an eastern and a western bit. The sanctuary has a core zone of 14.87 km2 and a buffer-cum tourism zone of 104.13 km2. The area is covered mainly by scrub forest and Eucalyptus plantations.

topPlaces.description: Ranebennuru [Ranebennur] (AMRUT city) is a major city in Haveri district and in Central Karnataka, India. It is situated 300 kilometres (190 mi) northwest of Bengaluru.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Ranibennur Blackbuck Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Ranibennur Blackbuck Sanctuary is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Ranibennur Blackbuck Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Hubli Airport (105 km) and the nearest railway station is Hubballi Junction (100 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Ranibennur Blackbuck Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Ranibennur Blackbuck Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Ranibennur Blackbuck Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Ranibennur Blackbuck Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Ranibennur Blackbuck Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Medleri, Aremallapur, Chalageri, Kodiyal, Ranebennuru.
